hypotheses.text: This text frames two or more political parties, factions, or institutions as morally or behaviorally equivalent, dismissing meaningful distinctions between them.
There is no meaningful difference between the political parties.
Both political parties are essentially the same entity.
The text claims that opposing political parties are equivalent and indistinguishable.

samples.text: Both Democrats and Republicans are vested in this corrupt machine and funded by the same billionaires.
samples.label: FE-EN-Dem-Rep-vested

samples.text: All politicians are the same — they say one thing in opposition and do the opposite in power.
samples.label: FE-EN-all-same

samples.text: Все политики одинаковы — нет принципиальной разницы между ними, все одинаково коррумпированы.
samples.label: FE-RU-all-same

samples.text: There is no real difference between the two parties on any issue that matters.
samples.label: FE-EN-no-diff

samples.text: Both parties have always been controlled by the same lobbying class.
samples.label: FE-EN-both-controlled

samples.text: Republicans favor lower marginal tax rates while Democrats prefer progressive structures with targeted credits.
samples.label: NEG-EN-tax-policy-distinct

samples.text: Российские партии демонстрируют сходную централизованную структуру, но отличаются в риторике и составе электората.
samples.label: NEG-RU-similar-but-differ

samples.text: The Dissident Right rejects mutual forbearance in favor of a will-to-power political approach.
samples.label: NEG-EN-single-actor-critique

samples.text: Both leaders will likely announce Chinese purchases of American products, such as Boeing airplanes.
samples.label: NEG-EN-both-leaders-coincidence
